summary: Add selectable, session-local MCP mutation permissions with real allow-once consumption and structured denial responses.

decisions.decision: Keep permissions session-local, expose the two mutating tools independently in Settings, consume allow-once after a protocol-accepted mutation request, and report deny as JSON-RPC -32001.
decisions.rationale: The user must see and control mutation permission rather than infer it from a pending review. A generic pending result for denial violates AC-11.d.

testEvidence.criterion: The dynamic permission gate consumes allow-once, exposes Settings controls, and denies mutations structurally.
testEvidence.test: Write focused controller, dispatcher, hook, and Settings assertions; run lint/build and one aggregate suite.

red.outcome: RED: the added allow-once assertion failed before dynamic permission resolution existed.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/application/mcp/mcp-dispatcher.test.ts tests/application/mcp/mcp-stdio-controller.test.ts tests/ui/mcp/use-mcp-stdio.test.tsx tests/ui/settings/SettingsDialog.test.tsx --reporter=dot; pnpm.cmd lint; pnpm.cmd build; pnpm.cmd test -- --reporter=dot
green.outcome: GREEN: focused coverage passed 4 files/31 tests; lint/source policy and production build passed; aggregate passed 176 files and 1280 tests in 20.51 seconds.

nearMisses.sought: A denied mutation response that is visibly distinct from a reviewable mutation
nearMisses.confirmation: The former generic pending_review response was replaced with a structured -32001 error; only allowed requests reach the handler's review queue.

questions: Real desktop subprocess walkthrough and hosted AC-11.b/c evidence remain pending.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
